env.world: probability laws on the compact interval [0,1] (weak topology) — Mathlib measure-theoretic world
env.notes: bypass-justified: the entire law program lives over P([0,1]) with weak convergence, moments, pushforwards and convex moment images. Surveyed Causalean/Experimentation/DesignBased {Design.lean, FiniteDesignMeasure.lean, Optimality.lean, TwoStage.lean}: Causalean's world is FiniteDesign (a probability over a finite assignment space) with E/Var/Cov and IsOptimalOn — the wrong abstraction (no P([0,1]) weak-topology / moment-set machinery, no continuous saturation law nu). Model nu as `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure ℝ` supported on Icc 0 1 (or a measure with IsProbabilityMeasure on Icc), delta_x as `MeasureTheory.Measure.dirac`, m_r/V as integrals of polynomials, D_pbar as Set.Icc (-pbar) (1-pbar).

env.world: finite-population two-stage randomized saturation design on M equal clusters of size m (deterministic saturation vector on the 1/m grid)
env.notes: bypass-justified: the implementable design is a deterministic saturation vector pi : Fin M → ℝ on the grid {0,1/m,...,1} with mean pbar, and L_M(pi) is its empirical law (M^{-1} sum of diracs). The finite-attainability/rounding/empirical-law content is pure combinatorics over Fin M → ℝ and needs no probability structure on the assignment space. Surveyed Causalean/Experimentation/DesignBased {Design.lean (FiniteDesign, E/Var/Cov), TwoStage.lean (compound design), FiniteDesignMeasure.lean}: these model the randomization over assignments (a FiniteDesign over {0,1}^N), whereas our objects are the deterministic saturation vector and its empirical law — different abstraction. The estimator hat_tau_DM and its design variance enter ONLY through the gated Cai expansion (S-blocks do not re-derive them); tau is a fixed real estimand parameter. binds_sampling_model true: the two-stage saturation sampling model is named here but its variance is supplied by the Cai gate.

ass:equal-cluster-size.notes: Design restriction N = M*m with M clusters of common size m; modeling assumption (Prop on the design size data). Search trace: grepped Causalean DesignBased.Designs {ClusterRandomization.lean, Stratified.lean} for an equal-cluster-size predicate — found cluster-randomization designs but no reusable named Prop matching this exact partition restriction; build-inline as a one-line Prop.

ass:budget-interior.notes: 0 < pbar < 1; interior treatment budget, modeling assumption. Provisional D0.R add (pending approval) but treated as a faithful interior-budget hypothesis. One-line Prop.

ass:isolated-partial-interference.notes: Partial-interference restriction (cluster j outcomes depend only on within-cluster assignments). Modeling assumption. Search trace: Causalean/Experimentation/HudgensHalloran {Basic.lean, Stratified.lean} models two-stage partial interference but at the FiniteDesign/exposure abstraction, not a reusable saturation-share Prop; build-inline.

ass:anonymous-share-specialization.notes: Within-cluster variance contribution depends on the assignment only through the saturation share u; novel modeling assumption. One-line Prop.

ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-mod.notes: Affine-in-centered-share homogeneous outcome working model; novel modeling assumption (provisional D0.R add). One-line Prop.

ass:cai-leading-expansion-domain.notes: Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi (2022) Theorem 9 regularity regime (M,m -> infinity, bounded linear-interference parameters, block-fixed isolated-cluster specialization). This is the declared ass: node (a modeling restriction naming the asymptotic domain); kept as a threaded hypothesis. The Cai THEOREM itself is the separate node lem:cai-randomized-saturation-moment-expansion (substrate-gate). Do not discharge/escalate this ass: node. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt6] Restored the dropped FULL Cai expansion domain: the prior Lean realization kept only `Mseq,mseq -> infinity`. Signature is now `CaiLeadingExpansionDomain (pbar) (Mseq mseq : N -> N) (Yseq : (n) -> Fin(Mseq n) -> Fin(mseq n) -> ... -> R)` with two added conjuncts the note states: (i) BOUNDED PARAMETERS `exists B, forall n j i z, |Yseq n j i z| <= B` (uniform boundedness of the linear-interference potential-outcome parameters), and (ii) REGULARITY in the homogeneous/block-fixed isolated-cluster specialization `forall n, IsolatedPartialInterference (Yseq n) /\ AnonymousShareSpecialization (Yseq n) /\ HomogeneousLinearShareWorkingModel pbar (Yseq n)`. Consumers (lem:variance-moment-reduction, prop:design-optimal-saturation) gain a `Yseq` parameter and pass `CaiLeadingExpansionDomain pbar Mseq mseq Yseq`; the gate def CaiRandomizedSaturationMomentExpansion threads `Yseq` into its `CaiLeadingExpansionDomain` antecedent.

def:centering-map.notes: T_pbar(u) = u - pbar. Trivial construction; def : ℝ → ℝ.

def:centered-support-domain.notes: D_pbar = Set.Icc (-pbar) (1-pbar). Construction with input pbar.

def:admissible-law-class.notes: CLASS (by_member_properties=[ass:budget-interior]) → structure bundling the budget-interior member Prop together with the defining mean-constraint field (integral u dnu = pbar) and support-in-[0,1]. N(pbar) realized as {nu // AdmissibleLaw pbar nu} or a membership predicate. P2: members == core by_member_properties.

def:fixed-second-moment-slice.notes: N_s(pbar,s) = {nu in N(pbar) : m_2(nu) = s}. Construction (inputs pbar,s).

def:centered-moments.notes: m_r(nu) = ∫ (u - pbar)^r dnu, r ∈ {2,3,4}. Construction (inputs nu,pbar).

def:variance-functional.notes: V(nu) = V_0 + V_1 m_2 + V_3 m_3 + V_4 (m_4 - m_2^2). Construction over centeredMoment.

def:profile-value.notes: F(s) = inf over N_s(pbar,s) of ∫ (V_1 d^2 + V_3 d^3 + V_4 d^4) dnu. Construction via sInf/iInf of the integral functional.

def:quartic-dual-residual.notes: Q_{a,b,c}(d) = V_1 d^2 + V_3 d^3 + V_4 d^4 - a - b d - c d^2. Polynomial construction.

def:outer-certificate.notes: H(s) = V_0 + F(s) - V_4 s^2 (the profiled outer value; the affine G^lin is only a sufficient lower-certificate). Construction over profileValue. G_{a,c} symbol also defined here as the affine certificate function.

def:implementable-vector-class.notes: CLASS (by_member_properties=[ass:equal-cluster-size, ass:budget-interior]) → structure bundling the equal-cluster-size and budget-interior member Props with the defining fields: pi : Fin M → ℝ, each coordinate in {0,1/m,...,1}, and M^{-1} Σ pi_j = pbar. Pi_{M,m}(pbar) realized as this structure's carrier set. P2 satisfied.

def:empirical-law.notes: L_M(pi) = M^{-1} Σ_j dirac(pi_j). Construction (inputs pi,M,delta_x) as a ProbabilityMeasure.

def:grid-split-rounding.notes: Grid-split rounding handle: mass-round (largest-remainder), grid-snap each support point via floor, final unit-step balancing to enforce mean pbar. Construction producing pi_rd from a K-atomic nu (inputs nu,M,m,pbar). Concrete combinatorial def consumed by lem:finite-atomic-grid-split-rounding. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt3] Restored the omitted construction content: `gridSplitRounding (M m : ℕ) (pbar) {K} (w u : Fin K → ℝ)` now builds π_rd from the atomic-law data via the three documented stages. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt8] Corrected Stage 2/3 to the paper's grid-SPLIT handle: Stage 2 is now ADJACENT-GRID SPLITTING WITH MIXING WEIGHTS (error-feedback split of each atom value u_ℓ between the two adjacent grid units ⌊m·u_ℓ⌋ and ⌈m·u_ℓ⌉, with a frac(m·u_ℓ) mixing share to the upper point, preserving each atom's mean contribution up to 1/m), and Stage 3 is MULTI-COORDINATE unit-step BALANCING (the integer grid-unit deficit D=round(N·pbar)−∑unit spread as single ±1/m steps across the first |D| clusters) — replacing the prior floor-snap + one-coordinate (cluster 0) correction, which was not the paper's grid-split construction. Signature unchanged, so all consumers (lem:finite-atomic-grid-split-rounding, thm:rounding-loss, thm:finite-attainability, prop:design-optimal-saturation, thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm) keep their statements byte-for-byte.

def:branch-map-contact-handle.notes: Notation-only handle: the finite support/contact families E_k(theta) (k=1,2,3) and branch objectives R_k^0 (S1 singleton {0}, S2 two-point {-b,a}, S3 three-point {-p,r,1-p}). Construction (inputs pbar,V_1,V_3,V_4). NOT a parameter-uniform partition. D0.R provisional definition change (def:branch-map-contact-handle); models the finite branch families used by thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm.

lem:variance-moment-reduction.notes: Optimizer-level bridge. IN-SCOPE conditional on the threaded Cai gate (lem:cai-randomized-saturation-moment-expansion supplies the o(N^{-1})-free expansion = V(L_M(pi))); the remainder (weak-convergence moment continuity, lower bound, boundary branch, rounding upper bound via thm:rounding-loss + prop:support-at-most-three) is built over Mathlib. Gate-consumer rule: difficulty is the gated Cai engine, so prove conditional on the gate, do not defer. [F2 scaffold-redirect] Restored the dropped FINITE leading-comparison equality clause: added a `designVar : (n) → (Fin (Mseq n) → ℝ) → ℝ` parameter and a leading conjunct `∃ rem, (rem·N → 0) ∧ ∀ n feasible π, designVar n π = V(empiricalLaw) + rem n` (the o(N^{-1})-free leading comparison equals exactly V(L_M(π))), provable from the threaded Cai gate. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt3] Added `(V2 : ℝ)` and threaded the BOUND Cai gate `hcai : CaiRandomizedSaturationMomentExpansion Yfull pbar Mseq mseq designVar V0 V1 V2 V3 V4` so the coefficients used to build `V` are the same tuple the Cai specialization binds. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt6] Added a `Yseq` design-sequence parameter and pass the FULL domain `hDom : CaiLeadingExpansionDomain pbar Mseq mseq Yseq` (now carrying bounded-parameters + regularity); `hcai` call site gains the `Yseq` argument.

thm:global-certificate.notes: Necessary-and-sufficient global dual/KKT certificate for the nonconvex quartic moment program. Pure P([0,1]) statement; build-inline over lem:centered-pushforward-program, lem:profile-value-reduction, lem:bounded-moment-slice-duality, lem:second-moment-slices-nonempty. The paper's own crux content.

prop:support-at-most-three.notes: Finite-atomic (≤3 support) optimizer reduction. IN-SCOPE conditional on the threaded extreme-point gate (lem:extreme-moment-slice-support, Winkler-1988 extreme points of 3-moment slices). Gate-consumer rule: the only hard engine is the gated extreme-point geometry; the assembly (boundary branch via lem:boundary-admissible-law-singleton, slice reduction via lem:profile-value-reduction + lem:compact-convex-moment-slice + lem:centered-pushforward-program) is built over Mathlib. Prove conditional on the gate.

prop:positive-penalty-threshold.notes: Closed iff threshold for delta_pbar global optimality. Build-inline over lem:pearson-centered-moment-bound, lem:two-point-centered-objective (and thm:global-certificate for the necessary direction). Pure algebra over moments.

prop:skewed-witness.notes: Concrete numerical witness nu_w=(2/3)delta_0+(1/3)delta_1 at pbar=1/3, V=(1,-10,1). Trivial: compute centered moments of a 2-atom law and norm_num. Build-inline.

thm:finite-attainability.notes: Exact denominator-compatible realization L_M(pi)=nu and the Bernoulli/stratified specializations. Build-inline: list n_ell=M w_ell copies, check grid membership and mean. Combinatorics over Fin M → ℝ.

thm:rounding-loss.notes: O(M^{-1}+m^{-1}) rounding loss bound. Build-inline by composing lem:finite-atomic-grid-split-rounding (coupling cost) with lem:quartic-variance-coupling-lipschitz (Lipschitz of V along couplings).

prop:sutva-corner.notes: SUTVA degeneration V_3=V_4=0. Build-inline over lem:centered-second-moment-extrema and thm:finite-attainability. (Core depends_on omits ass:budget-interior despite used_by; hyps follow depends_on per P4.)

oeq:full-branch-optimizer-map.notes: OPEN-ENDED QUESTION (kind openendedquestion; d0_open_oeq_residuals marks it the acknowledged open atlas-compression problem). Mapped to a named Prop def STATING the residual (existence of a parameter-uniform human-readable symbolic atlas over Theta), NOT a theorem. EXCLUDED from the theorems manifest; no theorem depends_on it. The delivered constructive content lives in thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm / lem:explicit-profile-value / prop:explicit-two-point-cell; only the atlas-compression residual is stated here.

prop:design-optimal-saturation.notes: Design-level payoff: assembles lem:variance-moment-reduction (carries the Cai gate transitively), prop:support-at-most-three (carries the extreme-point gate transitively), thm:rounding-loss, thm:global-certificate, prop:positive-penalty-threshold, prop:sutva-corner, lem:boundary-admissible-law-singleton. IN-SCOPE conditional on the gates threaded through its dependencies; no own un-gated open content. [F2 sync-back] Gates CaiRandomizedSaturationMomentExpansion + ExtremeMomentSliceSupport threaded as inline Prop hypotheses (carried transitively from variance_moment_reduction / support_at_most_three); both consumers realize their gate nodes as threaded Prop defs, not discharged lemmas. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt3] Added `designVar` and `(V2 : ℝ)` params; `hcai` now the BOUND gate form (carried transitively from variance_moment_reduction). [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt6] Added a `Yseq` parameter and pass the FULL `CaiLeadingExpansionDomain pbar Mseq mseq Yseq` (bounded-parameters + regularity); `hcai` call site gains the `Yseq` argument.

lem:explicit-profile-value.notes: Closed-form profile F(s)=min of two quadratic-interval minima (Phi_2 over I_2(s), Phi_3 over I_3(s)); H(s)=V_0+F(s)-V_4 s^2. Heavy but elementary: build-inline over lem:fixed-second-moment-envelope, lem:centered-pushforward-program, def:profile-value, def:outer-certificate. No gate. [F2 scaffold-redirect] Restored the dropped BRANCH-ATTAINER formulas: added two conjuncts over s∈Ioo(0,C). (i) first-min attainer at some t∈I_2(s) realized by the two-point centered law on the roots of x²-tx-s (pinned via cardSupportLe 2 ∧ m_3=s·t ∧ m_4=s²+s·t²); (ii) second-min attainer at some r∈I_3(s) realized by the endpoint-interior law on centered {-pbar,r,1-pbar} (decentered support {0,pbar+r,1}, pinned via cardSupportLe 3 ∧ support⊆{0,pbar+r,1} ∧ integral=Phi_3(s,r)). Each also asserts the corresponding iInf is attained at that t/r.

thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorith.notes: Existence of a globally optimal nu_alg in N(pbar) with certificate, reduced to the finite branch families S1/S2/S3. IN-SCOPE conditional on the threaded semialgebraic gate (lem:semialgebraic-optimization-algorithms supplies the terminating exact CAD/Thom solver over compact semialgebraic branch sets). Gate-consumer rule: the CAD/Tarski engine is the gated, genuinely-infeasible part; the paper's own content (branch enumeration + 'branch union contains a ≤3-support global minimizer' via prop:support-at-most-three + lem:explicit-profile-value + lem:two-point-endpoint-reduction, and certification via thm:global-certificate) is assembled conditional on the gate. State the minimizer-existence-with-certificate form; the literal 'terminating exact algebraic procedure' character is abstracted into the gate.

prop:explicit-two-point-cell.notes: Closed-form optimizer over the singleton/two-point class via the two endpoint cubic branches g_L,g_R and their finite stationary candidate list. Build-inline over lem:two-point-centered-objective, lem:two-point-endpoint-reduction. The two-vs-three comparison is delegated to thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm (not part of this node). No gate of its own.

lem:finite-dimensional-supporting-hyperp.notes: Rockafellar finite-dim separation/supporting hyperplane. Build-inline as a thin wrapper around Mathlib `geometric_hahn_banach_open` / `geometric_hahn_banach_compact_closed` specialised to a compact convex set and a disjoint open convex ray in ℝ^4. Search trace: leansearch 'separating hyperplane compact convex disjoint open' → Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.SeparationHahnBanach family; adapt to the lower-boundary supporting functional.

lem:compact-support-probability-laws-wea.notes: Prokhorov on a compact interval: P(I) is weakly compact. Build-inline over Mathlib's compactness of `ProbabilityMeasure` on a compact metric space (CompactSpace instance / Prokhorov). Search trace: grepped Mathlib for ProbabilityMeasure CompactSpace / Prokhorov — present as an instance; wrap for I = Icc 0 1. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt3] Generalized from the fixed [0,1] set to an arbitrary compact interval: `probabilityMeasure_isCompact (a b : ℝ) : IsCompact {ν | ν (Icc a b)ᶜ = 0}`.

lem:bounded-continuous-integrals-weakly-.notes: Portmanteau: mu ↦ ∫ g dmu is weakly continuous for bounded continuous g. Build-inline as a wrapper around Mathlib `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_iff_forall_integral_tendsto` (weak-convergence characterisation). Search trace: leansearch 'weak convergence iff integral bounded continuous' → tendsto_iff_forall_integral_tendsto. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt4] Restored the dropped COMPACT-INTERVAL `I=[a,b]`: signature now `weaklyContinuous_integral_bddCont (a b : ℝ) (g) (hg : Continuous g) (hb : ∃ C, ∀ x ∈ Icc a b, |g x| ≤ C)` concluding `ContinuousOn (ν ↦ ∫ g dν) {ν | ν (Icc a b)ᶜ = 0}` (weak continuity on P(I)), instead of the prior global `Continuous` over ALL laws with a globally-bounded g — the note's `g:I→ℝ bounded continuous ⇒ weakly continuous on P(I)` form.

lem:continuous-function-attains-minimum-.notes: Weierstrass extreme value theorem. REUSE Mathlib `IsCompact.exists_isMinOn` (a continuous function on a nonempty compact set attains its minimum). Used throughout the attainment lemmas.

lem:moment-image-compact-convex.notes: Moment image C_f ⊂ ℝ^4 compact (continuous image of weakly compact P(D)) and convex (affinity of mu ↦ moments). Build-inline over lem:compact-support-probability-laws-weakly-compact + lem:bounded-continuous-integrals-weakly-continuous.

lem:bounded-moment-slice-duality.notes: Slice minimizer iff exists multipliers a,b,c with Q≥0 on D and Q=0 on supp. Build-inline over lem:finite-dimensional-supporting-hyperplane + lem:moment-image-compact-convex (separate (1,0,s,alpha) on the lower boundary from the open ray).

lem:extreme-moment-slice-support.notes: SUBSTRATE-GATE (Winkler 1988 / Pinelis 2012: extreme points of a probability moment set with 3 generalized-moment constraints {1,d,d^2} are supported on ≤3 atoms, plus an extreme minimizer of a continuous affine functional). External classical result NOT original to this paper, not in Mathlib, and cannot make the consumers vacuous. Threaded as a named Prop into prop:support-at-most-three. Search trace: leansearch/local_search 'extreme point moment set probability measure finitely many constraints atoms' → no Mathlib decl; Krein-Milman + Choquet exist but not the finite-atom moment-set extreme-point bound. Future SUBSTRATE_DEBT to build before banking. [F2 sync-back] Realized as a threaded `def : Prop` (NOT a `lemma := by sorry`); taken inline by its consumer(s).

lem:centered-pushforward-program.notes: Pushforward by T_pbar is a mean-zero law on D_pbar with matching centered moments; converse; 0≤m_2≤pbar(1-pbar). Build-inline over Mathlib change-of-variables (`MeasureTheory.integral_map`).

lem:profile-value-reduction.notes: On each slice, V = V_0 + ∫ f dmu - V_4 s^2 and H(s)=inf V. Build-inline over lem:centered-pushforward-program + def:profile-value/def:outer-certificate.

lem:compact-moment-program-attains.notes: V attains a minimum over N(pbar); F(s) attained on nonempty slices. Build-inline over lem:compact-support-probability-laws-weakly-compact, lem:bounded-continuous-integrals-weakly-continuous, lem:continuous-function-attains-minimum-on-compact-set.

lem:pearson-centered-moment-bound.notes: t=m_3/s ∈ D_pbar and m_4 - s^2 ≥ m_3^2/s. Build-inline via Cauchy-Schwarz (Mathlib `inner_mul_le_norm_mul_norm` / `integral_mul_le`) and a reweighted probability law.

lem:second-moment-slices-nonempty.notes: N_s(pbar,s) nonempty for s∈[0,pbar(1-pbar)] via the explicit mixture (1-λ)δ_0 + λ·(centered two-endpoint law). Build-inline (explicit witness).

lem:two-point-centered-objective.notes: Centered moments and V-value of a two-point mean-zero law {a,-b}. Build-inline: direct 2-atom computation + ring. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt4] De-existentialized the CONCRETE pushforward law: the note names ν as the pushforward under d↦pbar+d of the centered two-point law (mass b/(a+b) at a, a/(a+b) at -b). Signature now takes `(ν : Law)` PINNED by its defining identity `hν : (ν:Measure ℝ) = ENNReal.ofReal (b/(a+b)) • dirac (pbar+a) + ENNReal.ofReal (a/(a+b)) • dirac (pbar-b)` and concludes `IsAdmissible pbar ν ∧ m₂=ab ∧ m₃=ab(a-b) ∧ m₄-m₂²=ab(a-b)² ∧ V-V₀=ab(V₁+V₃(a-b)+V₄(a-b)²)`, instead of the prior `∃ ν : Law, …` which existentialized the concrete law.

lem:cai-randomized-saturation-moment-exp.notes: SUBSTRATE-GATE (Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi 2022, Theorem 9 + Corollary 12): the randomized-saturation design variance of the difference-in-means estimator expands as V_0 + V_1 μ_2 + V_2 μ_2^2 + V_3 μ_3 + V_4(μ_4-μ_2^2) + o(N^{-1}), with V_2=0 in the isolated-cluster specialization. Deep external design-based result NOT original to this paper; cannot make consumers vacuous. Threaded as a named Prop into lem:variance-moment-reduction. Search trace: surveyed Causalean/Experimentation/DesignBased {Variance.lean, CompoundVariance.lean, ProductVariance.lean, EdgeVarianceBound.lean, Optimality/Neyman.lean} and HudgensHalloran/Variance* — design-based Var/Cov machinery exists but NO randomized-saturation third/fourth-centered-moment variance expansion under linear interference; not found. Future SUBSTRATE_DEBT. [F2 sync-back] Realized as a threaded `def : Prop` (NOT a `lemma := by sorry`); taken inline by its consumer(s). [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt3] Gate def re-parameterized to BIND the scoped coefficient tuple: `CaiRandomizedSaturationMomentExpansion Yfull pbar Mseq mseq designVar V0 V1 V2 V3 V4` now takes the coefficients explicitly, asserts the isolated-cluster simplification `V2 = 0`, and states the full centered-moment expansion `designVar n π = V0 + V1 μ₂ + V2 μ₂² + V3 μ₃ + V4(μ₄-μ₂²) + rem n` over feasible π (was an existential over free coefficients, leaving the consumers' coefficients unbound). [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt6] Added a `Yseq` design-sequence parameter and threaded it into the `CaiLeadingExpansionDomain pbar Mseq mseq Yseq` antecedent (which now carries bounded-parameters + regularity).

lem:empirical-centered-moment-continuity.notes: Weak convergence of empirical laws ⇒ convergence of centered moments r=2,3,4. Build-inline as a direct application of lem:bounded-continuous-integrals-weakly-continuous to φ_r(u)=(u-pbar)^r. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt5] Restored the dropped paper [0,1]-support hypotheses: added `hpi : ∀ n j, piseq n j ∈ Icc 0 1` (empirical vectors in [0,1]^M) and `hνsupp : SupportedOn01 ν` (weak limit in P([0,1])), so the centered test functions (u-pbar)^r are bounded on the joint support per the note's `saturation vectors in [0,1]^M ⇒ nu in P([0,1])` form.

lem:centered-second-moment-extrema.notes: 0≤m_2≤pbar(1-pbar) with equality characterizations (δ_pbar lower, Bernoulli vertex upper). Build-inline via U^2≤U on [0,1] and a.e.-equality arguments.

lem:semialgebraic-optimization-algorithm.notes: SUBSTRATE-GATE and INFEASIBLE-to-build here (Tarski-Seidenberg quantifier elimination, Collins cylindrical algebraic decomposition, Thom encodings, Sturm/root isolation): a terminating exact procedure for finite polynomial optimization over compact semialgebraic sets returning algebraic sample minimizers + certificates. Per the feasibility discriminator this is a famous hard formalization needing a major upstream Mathlib contribution → GATE, never crux, even though it is the engine of thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm. Threaded as a named Prop into that theorem. Search trace: leansearch/local_search 'cylindrical algebraic decomposition / Tarski-Seidenberg / real closed field quantifier elimination decision procedure' → Mathlib has IsAlgClosed and some real-closed-field algebra but NO CAD / general semialgebraic optimization decision procedure; not found. Future SUBSTRATE_DEBT (large, likely upstream). [F2 sync-back] Realized as a threaded `def : Prop` (NOT a `lemma := by sorry`); taken inline by its consumer(s).

lem:two-point-endpoint-reduction.notes: Min over singleton/two-point laws = min of {0, g_L on [-p,A], g_R on [A,q]}. Build-inline over lem:two-point-centered-objective + monotonicity of the product ab on the feasible interval.

lem:compact-convex-moment-slice.notes: Centered slice {mu : mean 0, second moment s} is weakly compact + convex; affine functionals attain min. Build-inline over lem:compact-support-probability-laws-weakly-compact, lem:bounded-continuous-integrals-weakly-continuous, lem:continuous-function-attains-minimum-on-compact-set.

lem:fixed-second-moment-envelope.notes: Lower/upper m_4 envelopes at fixed (mean 0, second moment s), attained by the two-root law and the {-p,r,q} endpoint-interior law, with the explicit weights. Build-inline via nonnegativity of (X^2-tX-s)^2 and (X+p)(q-X)(X-r)^2 integrated under mu; algebra/positivity heavy but elementary. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt5] Restored the dropped EQUALITY cases: appended two conjuncts asserting attainment. (i) lower envelope m4=s²+s t² attained by a ≤2-atom centered law μ₂ (pinned via supp⊆D_pbar ∧ mean=0 ∧ m₂=s ∧ m₃=m₃(μ) ∧ cardSupportLe 2) — the two-root law on x²-tx-s=0; (ii) upper envelope attained by a ≤3-atom centered law μ₃ supported on the explicit {-pbar, r, 1-pbar} set with the same first three moments and the upper-envelope m₄ value (r=(m₃-(q-p)s)/(s-pq)).

lem:finite-atomic-grid-split-rounding.notes: Run gridSplitRounding on a ≤3-atom nu to get pi_rd ∈ Pi_{M,m}(pbar) and a coupling with average displacement ≤ 2K/M + 2/m ≤ 6/M + 2/m. Build-inline: largest-remainder mass rounding + floor grid-snap + unit-step balancing; W1-style coupling cost bookkeeping over Fin M. [F2 scaffold-redirect attempt3] Bound to the def + atomic data: takes `{K} (hK : K ≤ 3) (w u) (ν) (hνeq : ν = ∑ w δ_u)`, concludes for `πrd = gridSplitRounding M m pbar w u` with coupling cost ≤ 2K/M + 2/m.

lem:quartic-variance-coupling-lipschitz.notes: |V(eta)-V(nu)| ≤ (2|V_1|+3|V_3|+8|V_4|)·Δ for any coupling of transport cost ≤Δ. Build-inline via |g_r(u)-g_r(v)|≤r|u-v| on [0,1] and the m_2^2 bound.

lem:boundary-admissible-law-singleton.notes: pbar∈{0,1} ⇒ N(pbar)={δ_pbar}. Build-inline: a nonneg r.v. with mean 0 is 0 a.e. (Mathlib `integral_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg` style).

infrastructure_needed.description: Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi (2022) Thm 9 randomized-saturation design variance expansion in centered treatment-proportion moments (V_2=0 in the isolated-cluster specialization); substrate-gate threaded into lem:variance-moment-reduction.

Winkler (1988)/Pinelis (2012) extreme points of a probability moment set with constraints {1,d,d^2} are supported on at most 3 atoms, plus extreme affine minimizer; substrate-gate threaded into prop:support-at-most-three.

Tarski-Seidenberg / cylindrical algebraic decomposition / Thom-encoding terminating exact optimizer + certifier for finite polynomial optimization over compact semialgebraic sets; infeasible-to-build substrate-gate threaded into thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm.

feasibility_notes: The mathematical content is a quartic moment program over P([0,1]) — pure Mathlib measure theory / real analysis — so the env binds the law world to Mathlib `ProbabilityMeasure` (bypass-justified: Causalean's experimentation cluster is design-based — FiniteDesign/E/Var/Cov over finite assignment spaces, IsOptimalOn — the wrong abstraction for a continuous saturation law). I confirmed one direct reuse: lem:continuous-function-attains-minimum-on-compact-set -> `IsCompact.exists_isMinOn`; the remaining analysis primitives (Prokhorov compactness, Portmanteau, Hahn-Banach separation, change-of-variables) are thin Mathlib wrappers (build-inline). The bulk of the paper — global certificate, support-at-most-three reduction, positive-penalty threshold, skewed witness, finite attainability, rounding loss, SUTVA corner, explicit profile value, explicit two-point cell, and all moment-geometry/two-point/rounding helpers — is build-inline over Mathlib and is the paper's own crux content. THREE nodes are substrate-gates (defer_tier:true, gate:true, lean_kind:assumption, threaded as hypotheses, excluded from the theorems manifest): lem:cai-randomized-saturation-moment-expansion (Cai-2022 variance expansion, external), lem:extreme-moment-slice-support (Winkler/Pinelis extreme-point geometry, external), and lem:semialgebraic-optimization-algorithms (CAD/Tarski-Seidenberg, genuinely infeasible to formalize here, the explicitly-named gate-not-crux case). By the gate-consumer rule their three consumers (lem:variance-moment-reduction, prop:support-at-most-three, thm:constructive-optimal-design-algorithm) stay IN-SCOPE and are proved conditional on the threaded gates; prop:design-optimal-saturation inherits the gates transitively. No statement is dropped, so there is no deferred_conjecture. The open-ended oeq:full-branch-optimizer-map is mapped to a named Prop def (atlas-compression residual), not a theorem. Overall: formalizable now conditional on three threaded substrate-gates => needs-new-infrastructure.
